title: betula transsilvanica
intro: Betula transsilvanica, also known as the Transylvanian birch, is a species of birch tree native to the Carpathian Mountains in Romania. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of betulin and betulonic acid. The medicinal properties of Betula transsilvanica have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and respiratory problems, and may also have antioxidant and antiseptic effects. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used topically to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
